8:["$","li","3",{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":"$5:props:iconNode","className":"lucide-chevron-right text-stone-300 shrink-0","size":13}],["$","span",null,{"className":"text-stone-600 font-medium line-clamp-1","children":"Dual Monitor Setup Guide: How to Do It Right for Productivity"}]]}]
9:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 mt-4 shadow-lg border border-stone-100 dark:border-stone-800 mb-10","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 leading-tight mb-5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Dual Monitor Setup Guide: How to Do It Right for Productivity"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/mia-collins","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1494790108377-be9c29b29330?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Mia Collins","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Mia Collins"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Workspace Designer"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","8 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"December 27, 2025"}]]}]]}]
a:["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"A second monitor sounds like a straightforward upgrade. Done wrong, it creates more friction than it solves. This guide covers placement, matching, ergonomics, and whether dual monitors are actually right for your work."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"should-you-get-a-second-monitor","children":"Should You Get a Second Monitor?"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The assumption that more screen equals more productive is common but not always true. A second monitor increases surface area for information — which is only useful if your work genuinely requires referencing multiple sources simultaneously."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Dual monitors make clear sense for: software developers (code + documentation), video editors (timeline + preview), financial analysts (spreadsheets + data sources), and anyone who spends significant time copying between two applications."}}],["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For most writers, marketers, and managers, a single large monitor (32–34 inch) or ultrawide is a better choice. The mental cost of shifting attention between two screens — and the neck strain of turning repeatedly — often outweighs the benefit of the additional space."}}],["$","p","4",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"If you are unsure, try an ultrawide first. If you still find yourself wishing for more isolation between tasks, add a second monitor."}}],["$","h2","5",{"id":"matching-two-monitors","children":"Matching Two Monitors"}],["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Mismatched monitors are worse than a single good one. The difference in colour temperature, brightness, and resolution creates a jarring visual experience when you move content between screens."}}],["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The ideal</strong>: two identical monitors. Same model, same panel, same settings."}}],["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Acceptable</strong>: monitors with the same panel type (both IPS), similar size (within 4 inches), and the same resolution. Calibrate brightness and colour temperature to match."}}],["$","p","9",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Avoid</strong>: combining 1080p with 1440p, or IPS with TN. The visual inconsistency is immediately obvious and fatiguing over time."}}],["$","h2","10",{"id":"ergonomic-placement","children":"Ergonomic Placement"}],["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This is where most dual monitor setups fail. The default — two monitors side by side centred on the desk — means you are looking slightly off-centre all day, causing accumulated neck strain."}}],["$","p","12",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>If one monitor is primary</strong> (you use it 80%+ of the time): centre that monitor directly in front of you. Place the secondary monitor to the side at a 30-degree angle. You turn to it, not crane."}}],["$","p","13",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>If both monitors are used equally</strong>: position them so the gap between them is at your midline. Both screens angle slightly inward so your eyes are not moving to extreme periphery."}}],["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Height</strong>: both monitors should have their top edges at or just below eye level, with matching heights. A monitor arm for each is the cleanest way to achieve this."}}],"$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15"]}],"$L16","$L17","$L18"]}],"$L19"]}]
b:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
c:["$","$L1a",null,{"children":["$","$1b",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@1c"}]}]
d:["$","h2","15",{"id":"cable-management-for-dual-setups","children":"Cable Management for Dual Setups"}]
e:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Two monitors means twice the cables. Without planning, this becomes unmanageable quickly."}}]
f:["$","p","17",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A USB-C monitor that daisy-chains from your laptop (Thunderbolt 4 supports this) reduces two display cables to one. Otherwise: route all cables down the back of the monitor arms, along desk legs, and into a cable tray under the desk."}}]
10:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A USB-C hub or Thunderbolt dock is essential — connecting two monitors, keyboard, mouse, audio, and ethernet through a single laptop cable rather than a nest of individual connections."}}]
11:["$","h2","19",{"id":"the-alternative-ultrawide","children":"The Alternative: Ultrawide"}]
12:["$","p","20",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Before buying a second monitor, consider a 34-inch ultrawide. At 3440×1440 resolution, it provides the equivalent of two 27-inch 1440p monitors side by side — without the physical gap, the brightness mismatch, or the cable complexity."}}]
13:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The workflow for most knowledge workers on an ultrawide: browser on the left half, application on the right. For focused single-task work, maximise one window and the extra width disappears."}}]
14:["$","p","22",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The ultrawide costs approximately the same as two decent 27-inch monitors once monitor arms, hubs, and cables are accounted for. It is frequently the better choice."}}]
15:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For the ergonomic principles that govern monitor placement, see our <a href=\"/blog/ergonomic-home-office-setup-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">ergonomic home office setup guide</a>."}}]
16:["$","div",null,{"className":"mt-10 pt-8 border-t border-stone-200 dark:border-stone-800 flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3","children":[["$","span",null,{"className":"text-sm font-medium
